definition:
1. of fruit, cooked by the sun, ripe, mellow , Lat. mitis, (Herdotus Historicus), etc. 
2. metaphorically in vocative, mostly as a term of endearment, kind, gentle , ὦ πέπον my good friend , (Iliad by Homer); κριὲ πέπον my pet  ram, (Odyssey by Homer);—; in bad sense, soft, weak , (Iliad by Homer); ὦ πέπονες ye weaklings , (Iliad by Homer): —; μόχθος πέπων softened  pain, (Sophocles Tragicus), etc.: with dative, ἐχθροῖς π. gentle  to one's foes, (Aeschulus Tragicus) (ML)
